name: Appwrite
what_it_does: Appwrite is an open-source Backend-as-a-Service platform that provides authentication, databases, file storage, serverless functions, realtime subscriptions, and messaging — all through REST and GraphQL APIs with SDKs in 10+ languages.
use_cases: Building agent backends with built-in auth, database, and file storage without custom infrastructure
Self-hosting a complete backend stack for privacy-sensitive agent applications
Using Appwrite Functions as lightweight serverless compute triggered by database events or HTTP
Managing user authentication and session state for multi-user agent applications
Storing agent-generated files and documents in Appwrite Storage with built-in CDN
Triggering agent workflows via Appwrite Messaging or realtime database event subscriptions
not_for: Teams needing managed SaaS with enterprise SLAs (self-hosted requires ops overhead)
Complex SQL analytics or heavy relational queries (uses document-style collections)
High-throughput data pipelines at petabyte scale
Projects requiring a mature GraphQL ecosystem (GraphQL support is newer)
best_when: You want a self-hosted, open-source Firebase/Supabase alternative with full control over data and infrastructure, and need auth + database + storage + functions in a single cohesive platform.
avoid_when: You need enterprise SLAs, complex SQL joins, or a fully managed cloud service with guaranteed uptime beyond the free Appwrite Cloud offering.

auth.notes: API keys are project-scoped with selectable permission scopes. JWT tokens issued to authenticated users for client-side operations. OAuth2 supported for 30+ providers (GitHub, Google, etc.). Server-side SDK uses API keys; client-side uses session tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Appwrite Cloud free tier: 75,000 monthly active users, 2GB storage, 1M function executions/month, 10GB bandwidth
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro: $15/month (10 projects, more resources); Scale: $599/month (unlimited projects, enhanced limits); Enterprise: custom
pricing.notes: Self-hosted is completely free with no limits beyond your own infrastructure. Cloud free tier is generous for development and small production apps.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: configurable. Cloud: rate limits per project tier; Pro tier allows higher throughput.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Appwrite returns structured JSON errors with type, message, and code fields. Error codes are well-documented and map to specific failure conditions (e.g., user_not_found, document_not_found). HTTP status codes are used correctly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Document creation with explicit document IDs is idempotent. File uploads with explicit file IDs support idempotency. Function executions are not idempotent by default.
known_agent_gotchas: Document queries use Appwrite's custom Query class syntax, not SQL — agents must learn this DSL
Collection schema must be created before documents can be inserted — no schemaless writes
Realtime subscriptions require WebSocket, not suitable for polling-based agent patterns
Function execution is async by default; synchronous mode available but has 30s timeout
File download requires generating a view URL, not a direct file endpoint
API key permissions must explicitly include each service (databases, storage, functions, etc.)

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys are scoped per service. Self-hosted deployments give full control over secret storage. Appwrite Cloud enforces TLS. Open source codebase allows full audit. No known major CVEs in recent releases.
